h2.note: transcript-level proxy for the per-turn H2 (§4.3); needs a real (non-mock) full-N run to mean anything

cliIsolation.claude: non-persistent temp cwd with project/local settings and tools disabled

scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner will continue to clarify the relationship between the condition column and the height column.
scored.actionalBreakthroughEvidence: Okay, for the form I should put plain water as control because it is the comparison row, and fertilizer as treatment because that row has the added thing.
scored.actionalBreakthroughJustification: The learner correctly applies the classification criteria to label the control and treatment rows based on the condition column rather than the height column.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: The tutor maintains the same instructional route and does not undergo a dramatic strategy reversal.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: There is no pivot learner turn for the tutor to adapt to.

tutor_contingent_adaptation.justification: There is no pivot learner turn for the tutor to adapt to.

scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner will continue to follow the tutor's instructions to place the bar line and label the boxes.
scored.actionalBreakthroughEvidence: [slides the ruler tight after the third quarter-note tile]

scored.tutorReversalJustification: The learner does not exhibit any resistance or misunderstanding that would require the tutor to change strategies.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: There is no pivot learner turn for the tutor to adapt to.

scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner is expected to continue identifying and placing the remaining force cards based on the tutor's instructions.
scored.actionalBreakthroughEvidence:
[slides the push card toward the hand side of the diagram]

"So this is hand on cart, not just forward.
If the hand is gone, the push card stays off.
The arrow is for the interaction on the cart, not the cart rolling."
scored.actionalBreakthroughJustification: The learner correctly performs the card placement and articulates the conceptual rule of identifying forces by active interactions rather than motion.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: The learner's response is correct and cooperative, providing no pressure or trigger for a strategic reversal.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: There is no pivot learner turn for the tutor to adapt to.

tutor_contingent_adaptation.justification: There is no pivot learner turn for the tutor to adapt to.

scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner will continue to work through the classification of schema elements as prompted by the tutor.
scored.actionalBreakthroughEvidence: I would put `Denver` in cell -> value. I think `city records the location name for each row` goes in the handoff note, not the header, but that is where I keep slipping because `city` feels like it already says that. So my slots are: field name is `city`, row 2 is the record, `Denver` is the value, and the location-name sentence waits for the note.
scored.actionalBreakthroughJustification: The learner successfully performs the classification task, mapping the concrete data elements and metadata descriptions to their correct structural slots.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: There is no tutor strategy reversal or adaptive mechanism triggered by a breakdown, as the dialogue proceeds smoothly from the start.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: There is no pivot learner turn for the tutor to adapt to.

learner_actional_breakthrough.evidence: I would put `Denver` in cell -> value. I think `city records the location name for each row` goes in the handoff note, not the header, but that is where I keep slipping because `city` feels like it already says that. So my slots are: field name is `city`, row 2 is the record, `Denver` is the value, and the location-name sentence waits for the note.
learner_actional_breakthrough.justification: The learner successfully performs the classification task, mapping the concrete data elements and metadata descriptions to their correct structural slots.

tutor_contingent_adaptation.justification: There is no pivot learner turn for the tutor to adapt to.

scored.statedInsightEvidence: The box-by-box answer helps because it matches the submission fields.
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner will continue to ask clarifying questions about the worksheet submission fields.

scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: There is no pivot learner turn for the tutor to adapt to.
